bifocal.target: Causal Graph Edges for shodh-memory knowledge graph

tier_1_full: Causal pattern detection (NLP at ingestion) — novel NLP, runs on every remember_impl() call, false positives create wrong edges, high coupling to EntityExtractor path
Directed edge semantics in spreading activation — core retrieval path, spreading activation treats all edges as undirected, direction-awareness is fundamental behavioral change
tier_2_standard: Query-type routing for why/what-happened-after — extends existing QueryType enum and OntologicalIntent, moderate coupling
Trust weight calibration for causal edges — tuning constants in constants.rs, existing density-dependent weight system absorbs this
tier_3_light: RelationType enum expansion — additive enum variants, backward compatible by design (msgpack tag-based serialization)

edge_analysis.data_crossing: New enum variants serialized via MessagePack to RocksDB. Old data deserialization must handle missing variants.
edge_analysis.control_flow: match statements on RelationType throughout codebase: as_str(), MIF export/import, ontological filtering.
edge_analysis.failure_mode: Non-exhaustive match after adding variants. Rust compiler catches this since enum is not #[non_exhaustive].

fractal_analysis.pattern: Existing variants grouped by domain. Causal group already has Causes and ResultsIn at line 1003-1005.
fractal_analysis.mirror_test: CausedBy is inverse of Causes. LeadsTo overlaps with ResultsIn. Redundant at enum level.
fractal_analysis.scale_consistency: Other groups don't enumerate bidirectional pairs separately. PartOf/Contains is the one exception.

hazop.OTHER_THAN: Bidirectional causation (A and B cause each other) — directed edge model cannot represent mutual causation cleanly.
hazop.MORE: Adding 4 variants when 2 exist = 6 total causal variants. Semantic overlap creates classification ambiguity.
hazop.PART_OF: Partial causation handled by confidence field, but relation type doesn't distinguish partial from full.

boundary_1_relation_type_enum.critical_finding: Causes and ResultsIn already exist at line 1003-1005 of graph_memory.rs but are NEVER CREATED by any code path. Proposal partially duplicates existing unused infrastructure.

fmea.failure_mode: Redundancy: LeadsTo vs ResultsIn creates inconsistent edge types
Backward compat: old msgpack data with unknown variant

edge_analysis.data_crossing: Raw text from Experience.content -> pattern matcher -> RelationshipEdge with causal type. Currently only CoOccurs edges created via extract_cooccurrence_pairs().
edge_analysis.control_flow: Must integrate into graph_index_memory() path around line 1398-1450 in mod.rs.
failure_modes: False positive: 'I read because I enjoy it' — motivational not causal
False negative: 'The deploy broke staging' — no explicit causal keyword
Performance: regex on every ingestion adds latency to remember() hot path

fractal_analysis.pattern_break: Existing edge creation is co-occurrence based (entity pairs in same sentence). Causal extraction requires clause-level semantic parsing — different extraction paradigm.
fractal_analysis.scale_break: Co-occurrence is O(entities^2) per sentence. Causal extraction needs dependency parsing or clause boundary detection — complexity class jump.
fractal_analysis.mirror_test: cold_start_extract_entities() in ner.rs uses heuristic patterns. Causal extraction needs similar heuristic quality but causation is semantically harder.

hazop.NO: No causal cue but causation implied ('After the meeting, the team changed direction'). Pattern matcher misses implicit causation.
hazop.REVERSE: 'Despite X, Y happened' — anti-causal language misclassified as causal.
hazop.LATE: Pattern detection after entity extraction means entities might not exist for causal edge targets.
hazop.LESS: Low confidence threshold creates noise; high threshold misses valid causation.

fmea.failure_mode: False positive causal edges from non-causal because/after
Performance regression on remember() hot path

edge_analysis.data_crossing: RelationshipEdge has from_entity/to_entity (direction stored). But spreading activation (lines 271-308 graph_retrieval.rs) traverses bidirectionally.
edge_analysis.control_flow: spread_activation_from_seeds() iterates get_entity_relationships() returning ALL edges. No direction filtering.
edge_analysis.failure_mode: Direction-aware for causal but not others creates two code paths in tightest hot path.

fractal_analysis.paradigm_mismatch: Spreading activation is fundamentally undirected (Anderson & Pirolli 1984). Directed traversal for subset of edge types breaks theoretical model.
fractal_analysis.scale_issue: At 1 edge type, manageable. At N types with direction, spreading activation becomes a graph query engine — different abstraction.
fractal_analysis.existing_solution: match_pattern() at line 3484 IS the directed traversal tool. Separate from spreading activation for good reason.

hazop.REVERSE: Why queries should follow causal edges backward. But undirected spread ALREADY reaches cause from effect. Direction-awareness would RESTRICT, not enhance.
hazop.PART_OF: Only following causal edges for why queries misses co-occurrence paths that reach answer through different graph region.
hazop.MORE: Higher trust weight for causal edges amplifies false positive edges from Boundary 2.

fmea.failure_mode: Direction restriction reduces recall for why queries
Higher causal trust weight amplifies false positive edges
Two-path inner loop adds branching to hot path

edge_analysis.clean_extension: QueryType enum has 3 variants. Adding Causal follows pattern. classify_query() cascades: Attribute, Temporal, Exploratory default.
edge_analysis.integration: apply_query_type_weights() adjusts (semantic, graph, linguistic) triple. Causal would boost graph weight.

fractal_analysis.consistent: Each QueryType adjusts retrieval weights. Causal fits the pattern perfectly.

hazop.OTHER_THAN: Why queries might be exploratory, not causal. 'Why do you like pizza?' is preference.

phase_2_fractal_atam.core_pattern: Hebbian learning: neurons that fire together wire together. Edges strengthened through co-activation, weakened through decay, promoted through tiers. Fundamentally ASSOCIATIVE.
phase_2_fractal_atam.paradigm_assessment: Causal reasoning is INFERENTIAL, not associative. This is the deepest tension in the proposal.

consistency.macro: BREAK — Associative model vs inferential causal reasoning
consistency.meso: CONSISTENT — Edge types serve as labels on associative connections; causal labels are just metadata
consistency.micro: CONSISTENT — RelationshipEdge struct supports direction and typed relations already

pattern_breaks: Extraction: all current edge creation is co-occurrence. Causal extraction requires clause-level semantic parsing.
Traversal: spreading activation is undirected. Direction-aware traversal exists only in match_pattern().

atam_tradeoffs.tradeoff: Causal edge accuracy vs extraction cost
Higher causal trust weight vs false positive amplification
New query type routing vs classification accuracy

sensitivity_points: Causal confidence threshold: no ground truth to calibrate against
Trust weight multiplier: small changes amplify through multi-hop (exponential at distance)

challenges.claim: Causal edges improve retrieval for why questions
challenges.counter: Current system handles why through semantic similarity and co-occurrence graph paths. LOCOMO has no causal reasoning questions. LongMemEval has limited causal queries. No benchmark evidence.
'I went home because it was late' triggers detection but is temporal motivation, not event causation. Keyword-based detection has estimated 40-60% precision per linguistic literature.
Causes and ResultsIn exist in enum but are NEVER CREATED by any code path. No extraction code produces these types. Creation pipeline doesn't exist.

correctness.code_evidence: Causes/ResultsIn exist (line 1003) but CausedBy/LeadsTo overlap; keyword NLP misclassifies non-causal 'because'
correctness.structural_evidence: Causal reasoning requires inference; Hebbian model is associative

robustness.code_evidence: False positive edges amplified by trust weight (line 274-283 tier_trust); no calibration fallback
robustness.structural_evidence: RPN 336 highest risk; no ground truth mechanism

security.code_evidence: No auth/PII/injection surface; edge types are internal labels; RocksDB unchanged
security.structural_evidence: Additive-only enum change; no external attack vectors

performance.code_evidence: Pattern matching on every remember_impl() hot path; branching in spread inner loop
performance.structural_evidence: O(sentences) regex pass added to O(entities^2) cooccurrence; marginal but on critical path

data_integrity.code_evidence: 40-60% precision NLP; false edges persist through Hebbian strengthening into LTP
data_integrity.structural_evidence: Wrong edges harder to remove than create once strengthened

maintainability.code_evidence: Clean extension points for RelationType, QueryType, apply_query_type_weights()
maintainability.structural_evidence: Causal NLP is new subsystem with no test infra; 4 new variants overlap 2 existing

completeness.code_evidence: Causes/ResultsIn exist but no creation code; match_pattern() supports direction but spread doesn't
completeness.structural_evidence: Missing: calibration dataset, precision targets, benchmark integration

scalability.code_evidence: RocksDB CF append-friendly; density-adaptive weights handle denser graph
scalability.structural_evidence: New edge types increase density; existing system handles this

testability.code_evidence: No causal test cases in LOCOMO/LongMemEval; no extraction ground truth
testability.structural_evidence: Would need new benchmark dataset with annotated causal relationships

observability.code_evidence: ConsolidationEvent::EdgeFormed tracks edges; RetrievalStats captures traversal
observability.structural_evidence: No causal vs co-occurrence distinction in introspection reports

usability.code_evidence: Users don't interact with edge types; value surfaces only through improved why-query answers
usability.structural_evidence: No user-facing change; benefit indirect and unquantified

modularity.code_evidence: RelationType cleanly extensible; QueryType pluggable; apply_query_type_weights() single function
modularity.structural_evidence: Causal NLP could be separate module; direction-aware spread modifies core algorithm

quality.notes: Partial overlap with existing types; NLP precision gap

completeness.notes: Major gaps: extraction pipeline, calibration, benchmarks

utility.notes: No benchmark evidence for retrieval improvement

coherence.notes: Fits edge-type model but breaks associative paradigm

cohesiveness.notes: Clean extension points but novel subsystem required

elasticity.notes: Density-adaptive system absorbs new edge types

function.notes: Direction-aware spread conflicts with existing model

ease_of_use.notes: Transparent to end users; dev integration moderate

overlook.target: Causal Graph Edges for shodh-memory knowledge graph

type_system_coherence.analysis: RelationType enum already has Causes/ResultsIn (unused). Proposal adds 4 overlapping variants (CausedBy, LeadsTo, Precedes, Enables). 6 causal variants with fuzzy semantic boundaries creates classification ambiguity. No other relation domain has this density.

data_flow_completeness.analysis: Edge creation pipeline only produces CoOccurs edges (line 1437 in mod.rs). No code path creates Causes/ResultsIn despite existing in enum since original design. Proposal requires entirely new extraction pipeline that doesn't exist.

schema_backward_compat.analysis: MessagePack tag-based serialization handles new enum variants gracefully. RocksDB schema unchanged. Additive-only per design comment at line 980.

api_surface_consistency.analysis: MIF import/export already maps relation types (export.rs:535, import.rs:480). New variants follow established pattern. MCP tools unaffected (edge types are internal).

module_boundary_respect.analysis: Causal extraction during ingestion crosses module boundary: NLP detection (embeddings/ner domain) creating graph edges (graph_memory domain) during memory formation (memory/mod domain). Three-module coupling for one feature.

retrieval_accuracy_impact.analysis: No benchmark evidence that causal edges improve retrieval. LOCOMO has 0 causal reasoning questions. LongMemEval has limited causal queries. Higher trust weight for unvalidated edges could REDUCE accuracy by amplifying noise.

ingestion_fidelity.analysis: Keyword-based causal detection (because, therefore, as a result) has estimated 40-60% precision. 'I stayed because it was raining' is temporal-motivational, not event-causal. Anti-causal language (despite, although) creates false positives.

graph_traversal_correctness.analysis: match_pattern() already supports directed traversal (line 3484). Spreading activation is undirected by design. Proposal to modify spread conflicts with ACT-R theoretical basis. Using match_pattern() as post-filter is correct alternative.

decay_model_interaction.analysis: False causal edges subject to same Hebbian strengthening as valid edges. Co-retrieval strengthens edges regardless of type correctness. Once potentiated (LTP tier), false causal edges resist decay indefinitely.

query_classification_accuracy.analysis: Why-query detection straightforward (starts with 'why'). But 'why do you like X' is preference, not causation. classify_query() cascade (Attribute > Temporal > Exploratory) would need Causal inserted with precedence rules.

consolidation_interaction.analysis: ConsolidationEvent already tracks EdgeFormed, EdgeStrengthened, EdgePotentiated, EdgePruned. Causal edges would flow through same consolidation pipeline. No special handling needed.

ingestion_latency.analysis: remember_impl() is hot path. Adding regex-based causal detection after entity extraction adds O(sentences) pass. Current bottleneck is embedding generation (80ms+); causal regex adds ~1-5ms. Marginal but non-zero.

retrieval_latency.analysis: If using match_pattern() post-filter (not modifying spreading activation), retrieval latency unchanged for non-causal queries. Causal queries add one match_pattern() call. Acceptable.

storage_overhead.analysis: New edges stored in existing CF_ENTITY_EDGES column family. Each edge ~200 bytes. Even aggressive extraction adds <5% edge count increase for typical conversation memories.

memory_pressure.analysis: Causal extraction patterns are static (compiled regex). No additional heap allocation beyond edge creation. Entity cache already exists.

graph_density_impact.analysis: New edge types increase graph density. Density-dependent weight system (DENSITY_THRESHOLD_MIN/MAX, line 36-38 graph_retrieval.rs) adapts automatically. But higher density shifts weight toward semantic, away from graph — potentially negating causal edge value.

false_positive_propagation.analysis: CRITICAL: False causal edge created at ingestion -> strengthened through co-retrieval -> promoted to LTP -> given higher trust weight in spread -> amplifies noise in retrieval for all queries touching those entities. Positive feedback loop with no correction mechanism.

edge_type_confusion.analysis: 6 causal variants (Causes, ResultsIn, CausedBy, LeadsTo, Precedes, Enables) with overlapping semantics. Extraction code must consistently choose between them. Different choices for same relationship create duplicate edges or missed dedup in find_relationship_between_typed().

retroactive_correction.analysis: invalidate_relationship() exists (line 3663) but requires knowing the edge UUID. No mechanism to find and invalidate all false causal edges after discovering extraction error. Bulk correction would require graph scan.

information_hazard.analysis: Causal edges are internal graph metadata. Not exposed to end users through MCP tools. No PII/security implications. Worst case: wrong retrieval results, not information leakage.

trust_calibration.analysis: Proposal gives causal edges higher trust weight than co-occurrence. But causal edges are LESS validated (extracted by heuristic NLP) than co-occurrence edges (grounded in literal text co-location). Trust inversely correlated with evidence quality.

upgrade_path.analysis: Can start with keyword-based extraction (V1), upgrade to LLM-based (V2) without schema change. Edge types are stable. match_pattern() traversal is independent of extraction quality.

test_infrastructure.analysis: No causal reasoning test cases in any existing benchmark. No ground truth dataset for extraction precision. Would need to create annotated test corpus from scratch. Existing test infrastructure covers co-occurrence but not causal semantics.

documentation_burden.analysis: RelationType enum is self-documenting (line comments). Query classification cascade needs documentation update. Causal extraction heuristics need linguistic justification.

dependency_stability.analysis: No new external dependencies. Regex-based extraction uses std. All changes are internal to shodh-memory crate.

rollback_safety.analysis: Additive-only enum changes. Old code ignores new edge types. Causal extraction can be disabled by removing the call in graph_index_memory(). match_pattern() post-filter is opt-in per query type.

monitoring_readiness.analysis: ConsolidationEvent tracks edge lifecycle. But no metric distinguishes causal edge precision from co-occurrence precision. Would need per-type edge quality tracking in feedback.rs.

spike_log.description: Positive feedback loop: false causal edge -> Hebbian strengthening -> LTP promotion -> higher trust weight -> amplified noise. No correction mechanism.
No benchmark evidence for improvement. Higher trust weight for unvalidated edges may reduce accuracy.
Trust inversely correlated with evidence quality: causal edges get higher weight but are less validated than co-occurrence.
No causal reasoning benchmarks exist. No ground truth for extraction precision. Test gap is structural.

distortion_check.halo_effect: Performance cluster (7.2) inflated by storage/memory dimensions that are trivially unaffected. The real performance concern (ingestion latency) is moderate. No halo distortion detected — cluster score is genuine.
distortion_check.horn_effect: Safety cluster (4.8) pulled down by false_positive_propagation (3.0) and trust_calibration (3.5). These are legitimately the weakest points. No undue horn effect.
distortion_check.anchoring: Existing Causes/ResultsIn in enum may anchor toward 'infrastructure exists' framing. Corrected: existence of enum variants != existence of creation pipeline.
distortion_check.survivorship: Proposal focuses on successful causal detection cases. Adversarial phase corrected for false positive and anti-causal failure modes.

coherence_guard.cross_cluster_consistency: Structural (5.8) and Behavioral (5.4) align — both show moderate gaps. Performance (7.2) is genuinely high. Safety (4.8) and Evolution (6.2) show expected tension between novelty risk and extensibility.
coherence_guard.outlier_check: Performance cluster (7.2) is 1.4 sigma above mean (5.88). Justified by genuinely low resource impact of additive changes. No outlier correction needed.
coherence_guard.dimension_correlation: false_positive_propagation (3.0) correctly correlates with trust_calibration (3.5) and ingestion_fidelity (4.0) — same root cause (NLP precision) manifesting across clusters.

blockers: false_positive_propagation (3.0) — positive feedback loop with no correction
test_infrastructure (3.0) — no ground truth or benchmarks for causal reasoning

overloop.target: Causal Graph Edges for shodh-memory knowledge graph

blockers.description: False positive causal edges amplified through higher trust weight. RPN 336.
Keyword-based NLP 40-60% precision. False edges persist through Hebbian strengthening.
Positive feedback loop with no correction mechanism.
No causal reasoning benchmarks. No extraction ground truth.

dual_agent_divergence.point: Storage layer supports causality (from_entity/to_entity + match_pattern) but retrieval algorithm's theoretical foundation (undirected spreading activation, ACT-R) does not.
dual_agent_divergence.resolution: Use match_pattern() as post-spread filter instead of modifying spreading activation. Preserves ACT-R model while adding directional capability.

phase_1_feedback_harvest.critical_spike: Causes and ResultsIn already exist at line 1003-1005 of graph_memory.rs but are NEVER CREATED by any code path. Proposed CausedBy/LeadsTo duplicate these.

small.description: Add Precedes to RelationType; use existing Causes/ResultsIn; do NOT add CausedBy/LeadsTo/Enables
Causal edge policy: confidence floor (0.5) + accelerated decay multiplier for unconfirmed causal edges
QueryType::Causal variant + weight adjustment boosting graph channel

big.description: Implement causal_extraction.rs with anti-causal filtering, clause-level pattern matching, confidence scoring
big.roi: Addresses both BLOCKERs (Robustness, Data Integrity) and highest RPN items (336, 315, 210)

big.description: Co-retrieval confirmation gate: causal edges stay L1Working until co-retrieved, then normal promotion path
big.roi: Breaks positive feedback loop (Overlook spike: false_positive_propagation 3.0)

big.description: Causal extraction test suite with precision >= 0.75 target, anti-causal negative cases

feature.description: Post-spread causal chain filter using existing match_pattern() for why/what-caused queries

phase_25_avoidance_audit.avoidance_detected: Testability initially unaddressed. B3 added to close gap.

implementation_order.change: Add Precedes to RelationType enum; reuse existing Causes/ResultsIn instead of adding redundant CausedBy/LeadsTo/Enables
Implement causal_extraction.rs with clause-level pattern matching, anti-causal filtering (despite/although/regardless), confidence scoring per cue-word class
Add causal edge policy in add_relationship(): confidence floor of 0.5 for Causes/ResultsIn/Precedes + CAUSAL_EDGE_DECAY_MULTIPLIER constant (1.5x faster decay for unconfirmed)
Gate causal edge tier promotion: edges with Causes/ResultsIn/Precedes type stay at L1Working until confirmed by co-retrieval event, then follow normal Hebbian promotion
Create causal extraction test suite: positive cases (explicit cues), negative cases (anti-causal language), edge cases (motivational 'because'), precision target >= 0.75
Add QueryType::Causal variant detecting why/what-caused patterns; add weight adjustment in apply_query_type_weights() boosting graph channel by +0.10
For QueryType::Causal queries, run match_pattern() post-spread to find causal chains through Causes/ResultsIn/Precedes edges; boost memories on chain by 1.2x

B1.complexity_vs_value: High complexity (230 lines) but addresses both BLOCKERs and highest RPNs. Justified.
B1.simpler_alternative: LLM-based extraction more accurate but adds latency and dependency. Keep regex V1, LLM as upgrade path.

B2.complexity_vs_value: Moderate complexity, directly breaks positive feedback loop
B2.simpler_alternative: S2 alone is static; B2 is dynamic (learns from usage). Both needed.

F1_revised.interpretation_territory: Borderline — causal chain boost factor (1.2x) needs tuning
F1_revised.simpler_alternative: Could omit boost and just log causal chain presence. But without boost, no retrieval impact. Keep with configurable multiplier.

combined_risks: Triple-gated system (B1 extraction + S2 confidence floor + B2 promotion gate) may be overly conservative. Mitigation: S2 floor at 0.5 (moderate), let B2 dynamic validation do heavy filtering.
If extraction produces zero causal edges (empty causal subgraph), match_pattern() returns nothing — graceful degradation to baseline. Safe.
B1 + S2 + B2 all modify graph_memory.rs but touch different functions (enum, add_relationship, tier promotion). No merge conflicts expected.
phase_5_regressions_pitfalls.ordering_dependencies: S1 -> B1 -> S2 -> B2 -> B3 -> S4 -> F1' (strict: each depends on predecessor's types/module)

graph_memory_rs.reason: Different functions: enum vs add_relationship vs tier promotion

mod_rs.reason: Different functions: graph_index_memory vs co-retrieval tracking

test_gaps: S2 (confidence floor) needs unit test in graph_memory.rs tests — ~15 lines
B2 (promotion gate) needs integration test showing causal edges don't promote without co-retrieval — ~25 lines
F1 (match_pattern post-filter) needs test showing causal chain traversal works — ~20 lines

final_ranked_improvements.description: Consolidate enum: add Precedes, reuse Causes/ResultsIn
Causal extraction module with anti-causal filtering
Confidence floor + accelerated decay for causal edges
Co-retrieval confirmation gate for tier promotion

conditions: MUST implement B1 (causal extraction with anti-causal filtering) before any trust weight changes — without it, higher causal trust amplifies noise
MUST implement B2 (co-retrieval confirmation gate) to break positive feedback loop identified by Overlook (false_positive_propagation: 3.0)
MUST implement B3 (test suite with precision >= 0.75 target) before merging — no ground truth currently exists
MUST NOT add CausedBy/LeadsTo/Enables — reuse existing Causes/ResultsIn, add only Precedes
MUST NOT modify spreading activation to be direction-aware — use match_pattern() post-filter instead (preserves ACT-R model)
SHOULD start with S1 (enum consolidation) as foundation, then B1 -> S2 -> B2 -> B3 -> S4 -> F1'
verdict.rationale: The feature has genuine value (causal reasoning is a real capability gap) but the original proposal has two structural problems: (1) the extraction pipeline doesn't exist and keyword NLP alone has insufficient precision (40-60%) for persistent edge creation, and (2) modifying spreading activation to be direction-aware breaks the ACT-R theoretical model when match_pattern() already provides directed traversal. With the Overloop improvements applied (anti-causal filtering, confidence floor, co-retrieval confirmation gate, test suite), the projected score improves from 52.8% to 64.5% and both BLOCKERs are resolved. P2 because it requires significant new code (505 lines, 1 new module) with no immediate benchmark impact — current MRR work (P1 reranker) has higher measured ROI.
key_findings: Causes and ResultsIn already exist in RelationType enum (line 1003-1005 graph_memory.rs) but NO code path creates them. The feature is half-designed, never implemented.
Spreading activation is fundamentally undirected (ACT-R model). match_pattern() at line 3484 already supports directed traversal and should be used instead.

False causal edges have a positive feedback loop: created -> Hebbian strengthened -> LTP promoted -> higher trust weight -> amplified in retrieval. Co-retrieval confirmation gate (B2) breaks this loop.
No existing benchmark (LOCOMO, LongMemEval) tests causal reasoning. ROI cannot be measured until test infrastructure exists.
